codeReview.summary: The WorkBacklog implementation in backlog.rs correctly aggregates from all 4 sources: Plan tasks (add_plan_task), FailureDerived (add_failure_derived), SpecGap (add_spec_gap), and Improvement (add_improvement). Implementation includes proper auto-approval logic, deduplication via file overlap and Levenshtein distance, failure-derived cap (3 per original task), priority scoring (1-5), and decay function. All 22 unit tests pass.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The features.json shows workerSessionIds: [] and status: 'pending' for orch-backlog-aggregation, indicating no worker session was assigned through the normal flow. The implementation in backlog.rs was committed directly (4899f13) without a worker session, which is consistent with other backlog-related features in this milestone.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json: workerSessionIds: [], status: 'pending'. Git log shows commit 4899f13 added backlog.rs with 4-source aggregation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backlog.rs file was implemented using Rust coding conventions following the project's patterns (async_trait, thiserror, tracing for logging, chrono for timestamps, serde for serialization). No deviations from expected skill procedure were identified as the implementation predates the feature request.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Code follows conventions from AGENTS.md: uses thiserror::Error for errors, tracing for structured logging, serde for JSON serialization.

summary: PASS: The orch-backlog-aggregation feature is correctly implemented. WorkBacklog in backlog.rs properly aggregates from all 4 required sources: Plan (add_plan_task), FailureDerived (add_failure_derived), SpecGap (add_spec_gap), and Improvement (add_improvement). Implementation includes: auto-approval for Plan/FailureDerived; manual approval required for SpecGap/Improvement; deduplication via file overlap (50%+ threshold) and Levenshtein description similarity (85%+); failure-derived cap of 3 per original task; priority scoring 1-5; decay function to raise approval threshold as run progresses. All 22 unit tests pass. Build succeeds with no errors or clippy warnings.
